summary: Implemented two previously stubbed/placeholder itMatadogas functions and added the item-specific variable and attribute structs needed to type them. The PR replaced an expanded hand-written JObj rotation/assert sequence with the correct HSD_JObjSetRotationY inline call after review, tightened timer decrement code in it_802CB2B0, and updated itmatadogas.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures.
what_changed: Added itMatadogas_ItemVars in itCommonItems.h with pad_0[0x60] and fields x60, x64, x68 at the documented item variable offsets.
Added itMatadogasAttributes in itCommonItems.h with four f32 fields x0, x4, x8, and xC.
Added itMatadogas_ItemVars matadogas to Item::xDD4_itemVar in src/melee/it/types.h.

Implemented it_802CAFD4(Item_GObj*): fetches Item and HSD_JObj, reads special attributes, initializes xDAC_itcmd_var0, matadogas.x60, and matadogas.x64, calls it_80279CDC, it_802CB350, HSD_JObjSetRotationY(jobj, 0.0f), and Item_8026AE84(item, 0x2715, 0x7f, 0x40).

Implemented it_802CB2B0(Item_GObj*): when xDAC_itcmd_var0 is nonzero, decrements matadogas.x60, resets it from attrs->x4 at zero, randomly chooses between two it_802CB4F0 calls using HSD_Randi(2), passing IDs 0xC1/0xC2 and attrs->x8/attrs->xC.
Updated itmatadogas.h prototypes: it_802CB2B0 became void it_802CB2B0(Item_GObj* gobj), and it_802CB4F0 became void it_802CB4F0(Item_GObj*, s32, f32).
smart_moves: Moved Matadogas-specific per-item state into the xDD4_itemVar union instead of continuing to use raw offsets or generic unknown storage.
Used the item article special attributes to type repeated timer and parameter accesses in both initialization and update logic.
After review, collapsed a manually reconstructed jobj.h assert/dirty-matrix sequence into the project inline HSD_JObjSetRotationY(jobj, 0.0f), improving readability and matching maintainability.
Used pre-decrement in the timer condition, matching reviewer guidance and avoiding an unnecessary temporary timer variable.
Added a focused random header include instead of relying on implicit declarations for HSD_Randi.
decomp_lessons: When an apparent function body contains asserts against jobj.h line numbers and direct JObj rotation writes, check whether it is actually a baselib/jobj.h inline expansion. Convert the hexadecimal assert line number to decimal and search the header; here 0x294 became line 660 and identified HSD_JObjSetRotationY.
For timer countdown patterns, prefer compact mutation forms like if (--field == 0) when they match and reduce extra temporaries.

If special attributes are floats, avoid unnecessary casts when assigning them if the target type/matching context allows; reviewer specifically suggested assigning attrs->x4 directly to matadogas.x60 in the final diff.
Typed item-variable structs can unlock cleaner decompilation of item logic by replacing xDD4 raw accesses with named item-specific union members.
Prototype cleanup in item headers should accompany C implementation work so future functions can call the symbols with correct argument and return types.
naming_conventions: Item variable struct name follows item CamelCase plus suffix: itMatadogas_ItemVars.
Special-attribute struct name follows item CamelCase plus suffix: itMatadogasAttributes.
The xDD4_itemVar union member uses lowercase item name: matadogas.
Unknown Matadogas functions retained address-based names such as it_802CAFD4, it_802CB2B0, and it_802CB4F0 where semantic names were not established.
Motion callbacks remain descriptive existing names such as itMatadogas_UnkMotion1_Coll and itMatadogas_UnkMotion2_Coll.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Recognize jobj.h inline expansions by __assert file/line references; convert hex line numbers to decimal and search the header for the invoked inline.
Use HSD_JObjSetRotationY rather than open-coding flag checks, quaternion asserts, rotate.y assignment, and HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub behavior.
Use PAD_STACK(8) in it_802CB2B0, indicating a stack-padding requirement for matching in this function.
Use HSD_Randi(2) for a two-way random branch selecting between two effect/action IDs, 0xC1 and 0xC2.
Use prefix decrement on the timer field to match reviewer-suggested codegen shape.
review_feedback: ribbanya identified the expanded JObj rotation code as a jobj.h inline and suggested replacing it with HSD_JObjSetRotationY(jobj, 0.0f).
ribbanya explained the tactic: convert a hex assert line number to decimal and search jobj.h; 0x294 corresponds to decimal 660 in this case.
ribbanya suggested simplifying the countdown code to if (--ip->xDD4_itemVar.matadogas.x60 == 0).
ribbanya suggested assigning ip->xDD4_itemVar.matadogas.x60 = attrs->x4 instead of using an explicit temporary/cast form in the shown hunk.
ribbanya suggested formatting the itMatadogasAttributes typedef with a blank line after the closing typedef.
ribbanya pointed back to the JObj inline comment to remove the custom static inline helper that duplicated baselib behavior.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for itMatadogas_ItemVars fields x60/x64/x68 to see whether they received semantic names.
Search for other PR review comments mentioning jobj.h inline or HSD_JObjSetRotationY to build a reusable inline-identification rule.
Inspect it_802CB4F0 implementation and callsites to determine semantic names for the 0xC1 and 0xC2 actions/effects.
Check whether attrs->x0/x4/x8/xC later gained names in item attribute documentation.
